zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Atkyns於1530年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Sir William Molyneux47歲，母親Lady Jane Rugge56歲。她於1551年11月8日與Adam Thomas Bland在英國英格蘭倫敦結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1596年7月10日在英國倫敦享年66歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Atkyns于1530年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Sir William Molyneux47岁，母亲Lady Jane Rugge56岁。她于1551年11月8日与Adam Thomas Bland在英国英格兰倫敦结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1596年7月10日在英国倫敦享年66岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Atkyns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Atkin (a pet form of Adam) with genitival -s, or a variant of Atkin , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Atkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Atkin (composed of Ade + the diminutive suffix -kin). This is one of the many medieval pet forms of Adam . Compare Scottish Aitken .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Atkyns was born in 1530, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Sir William Molyneux, was 47 and her mother, Lady Jane Rugge, was 56. She married Adam Thomas Bland on 8 November 1551, in London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 10 July 1596, in London, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Atkyns est née en 1530, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Molyneux, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Lady Jane Rugge, avait 56 ans. Elle a épousé Adam Thomas Bland le 8 novembre 1551, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 juillet 1596, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Atkyns 1530 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Molyneux 47 und ihre Mutter Lady Jane Rugge 56. Sie hat am 8. November 1551 in London, England, United Kingdom Adam Thomas Bland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. Juli 1596 im Alter von 66 Jahren in London, England und wurde in London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Atkyns è nata il nel 1530 nel London, Middlesex, England, sua padre, Sir William Molyneux, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Lady Jane Rugge, aveva 56 anni. Lei sposato Adam Thomas Bland il 8 novembre 1551, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 10 luglio 1596, nel London, England, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Atkynsが1530年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir William Molyneuxは47歳，母Lady Jane Ruggeは56歳であった。1551年11月8日にイギリスイギリスロンドンでAdam Thomas Blandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1596年7月10日にイギリスロンドンで66歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Atkyns(이)가 1530년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Molyneux(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Jane Rugge(은)는 56세였다. 그녀는 1551년 11월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 Adam Thomas Bland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1596년 7월 10일 영국 런던에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Atkyns nasceu em 1530, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Molyneux, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Lady Jane Rugge, tinha 56 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam Thomas Bland em 8 de novembro de 1551, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de julho de 1596, em London, Inglaterra, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Atkyns родилась в 1530 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Molyneux было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Jane Rugge, 56. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam Thomas Bland 8 ноября 1551 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 10 июля 1596 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Atkyns nació en 1530, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Molyneux, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Lady Jane Rugge, tenía 56 años. Se casó con Adam Thomas Bland el 8 de noviembre de 1551, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 10 de julio de 1596, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
